name: Joel Farabee
summary: Joel Farabee is still working to solidify his place in Calgary’s lineup. He’s often used in depth roles but brings good skating and awareness, with potential to become a reliable contributor in time.

name: Jesperi Kotkaniemi
summary: Jesperi Kotkaniemi is a center who fits perfectly into his team's puck-dominant system. Whether he is on the ice or off, his team controls the flow of shot attempts at an elite level, making him an effective component of a relentless four-line attack.

name: Alexander Holtz
summary: Holtz has shown encouraging signs in limited action with Vegas. When he’s on the ice, the team tends to control play, suggesting strong underlying potential as he earns more ice time.

name: Jamie Oleksiak
summary: Jamie Oleksiak uses his massive frame to serve as a shutdown defenseman in a depth role. He is a high-end shot blocker, providing a steady, defensive-minded presence on the back end.

name: Phillip Danault
summary: Phillip Danault brings his trademark two-way consistency, blocking a high number of shots while also setting up teammates effectively. His primary assists and defensive commitment underline his value as a playmaker who contributes on both sides of the puck.

name: Tyson Foerster
summary: Tyson Foerster has a knack for generating second-chance opportunities around the net. His tenacity and quick release make him a valuable young scorer in Philadelphia’s offense.

name: Joel Edmundson
summary: Joel Edmundson is a physical, stay-at-home defenseman who provides veteran depth. He is used in a specific defensive role, and his team's strong possession numbers when he's on the bench reflect the strength of their top pairings.

name: Kurtis MacDermid
summary: Kurtis MacDermid serves as an enforcer and physical presence for New Jersey. His size and toughness provide protection and energy for a young roster.

name: Adam Henrique
summary: Adam Henrique shows veteran responsibility, ranking near the top of the league in blocked shots. His off-ice possession and expected-goals metrics underline how stable the team remains around him.

name: Colton Parayko
summary: Parayko continues to contribute offensively from the back end, chipping in goals and high-danger chances. His combination of size, skating, and confidence makes him a steady top-four option.

name: Juha Jaaska
summary: Juha Jaaska has shown strong puck possession and awareness in limited ice time. He fits Carolina’s fast-paced, structured system and could grow into a dependable depth winger.

name: Erik Cernak
summary: Erik Cernak is a tough, reliable defender who uses his size and physical play to protect Tampa Bay’s net. He’s steady in his own end and brings the kind of edge every team needs in the playoffs.

name: Tage Thompson
summary: Tage Thompson is a towering goal scorer with a lethal shot. His reach and quick release make him nearly impossible to defend, and he continues to be Buffalo’s most explosive offensive weapon.

name: Alex Ovechkin
summary: Alex Ovechkin remains one of the most dangerous goal scorers in the game. He fires pucks from everywhere, leading Washington’s attack with power and precision. Even as he ages, his knack for finding the net and generating offense is unmatched.

name: Vladislav Gavrikov
summary: Vladislav Gavrikov is a reliable stay-at-home defenseman who brings a calm, structured game. He rarely makes mistakes and helps stabilize the Kings’ blue line.

name: Alex Laferriere
summary: Alex Laferriere finds success near the net, generating second-chance opportunities and rebound goals. His possession metrics show he helps control play for Los Angeles, while his missed shots suggest he isn’t afraid to fire often from a range of areas.

name: Tanner Pearson
summary: Tanner Pearson’s veteran presence provides a steadying influence for Vegas. While not driving offense at his peak levels, his experience and smart play without the puck make him a useful depth piece.

name: Morgan Frost
summary: Morgan Frost is a creative playmaker with strong puck skills. He’s confident with the puck and helps generate scoring chances through his vision and quick hands.

name: Jack Roslovic
summary: Jack Roslovic’s speed and puck control fit well within Carolina’s up-tempo style. He helps drive possession and transition play, giving the Hurricanes extra pace down the middle.

name: Mikko Rantanen
summary: Mikko Rantanen is a complete offensive threat who can score from anywhere. His blend of power and finesse shows through his strong goal and assist numbers, as well as his high shot volume. He’s dangerous around the crease, creating rebounds and high-danger looks regularly. Though occasionally reckless with the puck, his ability to tilt the ice in his team’s favor is undeniable.

name: Matthew Tkachuk
summary: Matthew Tkachuk is one of the most unique and dominant players in the NHL. He is a truly generational possession driver who controls the game like few others, combining his puck dominance with an elite ability to create chaos and scoring chances around the net.

name: Mason Marchment
summary: Mason Marchment brings edge and emotion, sitting high in the league for penalty minutes. His aggressive forechecking defines his game.

name: Nikita Zadorov
summary: Nikita Zadorov is one of the league’s most intimidating physical defensemen. He hits hard, plays with an edge, and keeps opponents honest while adding grit and toughness to Boston’s blue line.

name: William Carrier
summary: William Carrier is a perfect cog in a dominant machine. He is a possession-driving fourth liner who helps his team control the puck when he's on the ice, and his excellent off-ice metrics show the team continues that dominance with its other lines.

name: Jack McBain
summary: Jack McBain plays a bruising, north-south style of hockey. He hits everything in sight and makes life tough for defenders. While his offense is still developing, his energy and size make him an important depth piece.

name: Nick Suzuki
summary: Nick Suzuki continues to shine as Montreal’s top center. His vision and playmaking set him apart, and he’s capable of both creating and finishing plays. While he takes risks with the puck, his composure and consistency make him the heartbeat of the Canadiens’ offense.

name: Luke Schenn
summary: Luke Schenn remains one of hockey’s most physical defenders, leading the league with 300 hits. A steady presence known for toughness and durability.

name: Zach Werenski
summary: Zach Werenski had a monster season, leading all defenders in shot attempts, points, and goals. His volume shooting and consistent offensive drive make him one of the NHL’s elite blue-line threats.

name: Scott Laughton
summary: Scott Laughton delivers steady physical play, ranking high in hits. He adds grit and energy to his team’s forward group.

name: Nathan MacKinnon
summary: Nathan MacKinnon is a superstar in every sense. His explosive speed, elite shot, and relentless drive make him nearly unstoppable. Whether creating for teammates or finishing himself, he’s the heartbeat of Colorado’s offense.

name: Jamie Benn
summary: Jamie Benn continues to play his trademark power-forward game. He mixes physicality with scoring touch and isn’t shy about playing on the edge to spark his team.

name: Evgenii Dadonov
summary: Evgenii Dadonov maintains consistent usage, logging heavy bench time as part of a deep forward rotation.

name: Brad Marchand
summary: Brad Marchand is the NHL's ultimate agitator, blending elite offensive talent with a uniquely irritating style. He is a master at both drawing and taking penalties, while also being highly effective at getting to the front of the net to generate high-danger scoring chances.

name: Connor Clifton
summary: Connor Clifton brings grit to Buffalo’s defense with over 200 recorded hits. His physical play sets the tone and keeps opposing forwards under pressure in the defensive zone.

name: Zach Hyman
summary: Zach Hyman has become one of the NHL’s premier net-front forwards. He scores gritty goals, battles through defenders, and brings nonstop effort that perfectly complements Edmonton’s stars.

name: Alex Vlasic
summary: Alex Vlasic is a promising young defenseman tasked with incredibly difficult minutes on a rebuilding team. Despite being on the ice for a league-high number of goals against, he excels individually with elite takeaway numbers and a strong commitment to blocking shots.

name: Mathew Barzal
summary: Mathew Barzal remains one of the most dynamic skaters in the NHL. His creativity and vision consistently drive the Islanders’ offense and open space for his linemates.

name: Jason Robertson
summary: Jason Robertson is an elite offensive winger and a prolific point producer. He is particularly adept at scoring on rebounds around the net and consistently puts up high-end goal and assist totals, even if it comes with the risk of turning the puck over.

name: Michael McCarron
summary: Michael McCarron brings physicality and grit to Nashville’s lineup. He plays a heavy game, drawing and taking penalties in equal measure. While not known for scoring, he’s a tough presence who wears down opponents in defensive roles.

name: Tyson Jost
summary: Tyson Jost brings energy and structure to Carolina’s lineup. He plays well within the team’s fast, possession-heavy system, helping drive play both on and off the puck.

name: Devon Toews
summary: Devon Toews is one of the NHL’s most dependable defensemen. He complements Colorado’s high-paced style perfectly, using smart positioning and clean puck movement to keep play flowing smoothly.

name: Jesper Bratt
summary: Jesper Bratt’s elite playmaking and speed make him one of New Jersey’s offensive cornerstones. His ability to create scoring chances for himself and others keeps defenses constantly guessing.

name: Erik Gudbranson
summary: Erik Gudbranson’s off-ice metrics suggest the team maintains solid control when he’s resting. A rugged defender known more for toughness than puck control.

name: Ryan Pulock
summary: Ryan Pulock is a steady, defense-first blueliner whose game is built around his commitment to team defense. His standout trait is his willingness to get in shooting lanes and block shots.

name: Jaden Schwartz
summary: Jaden Schwartz brings leadership and tenacity to Seattle. He’s aggressive on the puck, creates offense through hard work, and adds veteran stability to the Kraken lineup.
